paper_title: Fresh Tomatoes: Ideas to Build a Productive Eco-System
research_answer: Capturing just 1% of Germany's fresh tomato imports would increase Albanian export volumes by 53% — a striking demonstration of how large and high-value the German market is relative to Albania's current export base. This is because Germany is a major high-value importer (with average prices around or above $2/kg), while Albania currently exports negligible volumes to Western Europe. To meet this additional demand without transforming the entire agricultural system, Albania would need to expand greenhouse facilities by only 10% (requiring an investment of approximately $6 million) and reduce current post-harvest losses by two-thirds. Post-harvest losses currently run at about 20% and amount to roughly 3,500 tons — a 2/3 reduction in those losses alone would yield a 3% increase in usable production and a 15% increase in export volumes. Combined, these two relatively modest interventions could unlock the 53% volume growth implied by 1% German market penetration.
supporting_quotes: suppose Albania can get 1% of the fresh tomato imports of Germany. Ceteris Paribus... demand would be increased by about 7% in real terms, which in turn implied that exports would grow 53% in volumes

Just by increasing in only 10% the number of Ha with Greenhouse facilities (which requires an approximate investment of no more than $ 6 Million) and reducing by 2/3 the current losses due to lack of demand

Again, 2/3 reduction in those losses (which represent no more than 3.5 thousand Tones, less than 3% of total current production) would imply a 3% additional increase in production and a 15% increase in exports (in real terms)
the proportion of lost production is too high (about 20% on average according to our sample)
